div.p: Legend: • buf_ring: Provided buffer ring shared with kernel (bgid identifies it) • bid: Buffer ID stored in ring entry, returned in CQE flags • IOSQE_BUFFER_SELECT: SQE flag requesting kernel buffer selection • IORING_CQE_F_BUFFER: CQE flag indicating buffer was selected • Head/Tail: Ring indices managed by kernel (head) and app (tail for add) Critical: Buffer is "owned" by kernel during receive, then by app after CQE. App MUST return buffer to ring after processing or pool will exhaust!
Head → [1]: buf[1] @ 0x7f2000 [2]: buf[2] @ 0x7f3000 ... [15]: buf[15] @ 0x7fF000 ← Tail [0] CLAIMED by kernel
DMA Transfer: 2048 bytes Source: NIC RX ring buffer Dest: buf[0] @ 0x7f1000 (pinned) ✓ No kernel copy ✓ Zero-copy receive
0x7f1000: [R X D A T A H E R E ...] ↑ 2048 bytes received

flags = 0x00010000 Bit 0 (BUFFER): 1 ← Buffer was selected Buffer ID extraction: bid = flags >> 16 bid = 0x00010000 >> 16 bid = 0x0001 = 1 Wait... that's bid=1? Actually: IORING_CQE_BUFFER_SHIFT = 16 bid = 0x00010000 >> 16 = 1 But kernel used buf[0]! The bid in flags is the buffer ID stored in the ring entry, not index.

text.tspan: // Add buffer back to ring tail:
